name: yolo
description:
Browser automation skill for Lovable deployments. Activates when:
- yolo_mode: on in CLAUDE.md
- Running /deploy-edge or /apply-migration commands
- After git push when auto_deploy: on (automatic detection)
- Any mention of "yolo mode", "automate Lovable", "browser automation"

Automatically navigates to Lovable.dev and submits deployment prompts.
Runs verification tests based on configuration.
Auto-deploys after git push when enabled.
capabilities: Navigate to Lovable.dev and submit deployment prompts automatically
Detect backend changes after git push using DOM-based GitHub sync detection
Run 3-level verification tests (logs, console, functional) after deployment
Auto-deploy edge functions and migrations without manual commands
Extract existing secret names from Lovable Cloud settings during init
Provide manual fallback prompts when any automation step fails
author: 10K-Digital
securitySummary: Legitimate browser automation skill for Lovable.dev deployments. Static scanner flagged 672 issues due to markdown code formatting being misinterpreted as shell backticks. All 568 'external_commands' findings are FALSE_POSITIVES - they are markdown inline code syntax (`tool_name`) in documentation, not actual shell execution. Network URLs (lovable.dev) and config file reads (CLAUDE.md) are expected for deployment automation. A previous audit (skill-report.json) confirmed this skill is safe with low risk.
valueStatement: Lovable requires manual prompts for edge function and database migrations. This skill automates browser navigation and prompt submission after git push. Developers save time while maintaining safety with manual fallbacks on every failure.
limitations: Requires Claude in Chrome extension installed and logged in
Only works with edge functions and migrations (not tables, RLS, storage)
Lovable UI changes may break automation until skill is updated

useCases.title: Automate edge deployments
useCases.description: Deploy edge functions automatically after git push without manually running prompts in Lovable

useCases.title: Streamline migration workflow
useCases.description: Apply database migrations automatically with verification tests after git push

useCases.title: Init secret detection
useCases.description: During init, extract existing secrets from Lovable Cloud to avoid duplicate configuration

promptTemplates.title: Enable yolo mode
promptTemplates.prompt: Enable yolo mode for automatic Lovable deployments

promptTemplates.title: Deploy edge function
promptTemplates.prompt: Run /deploy-edge to deploy the send-email function with automation

promptTemplates.title: Auto-deploy after push
promptTemplates.prompt: Enable auto-deploy so Lovable deployments happen automatically after git push

promptTemplates.title: Debug automation
promptTemplates.prompt: Enable debug mode and run /deploy-edge to see detailed automation logs

bestPractices: Start with auto-deploy off until comfortable with yolo mode workflow
Keep testing enabled to catch issues before they reach production
Monitor first few auto-deployments to verify everything works correctly
antiPatterns: Enabling auto-deploy without understanding the workflow first
Skipping testing for critical production deployments
Ignoring automation failures without checking the manual fallback prompt

faq.question: What does yolo mode do?
faq.answer: Automates Lovable deployments using browser automation. After git push, it detects backend changes and submits deployment prompts automatically.

faq.question: Is browser automation safe?
faq.answer: Yes. Yolo mode requires explicit opt-in via CLAUDE.md, provides manual fallback prompts on failure, and never modifies code without user intent.

faq.question: What browsers are supported?
faq.answer: Requires Claude in Chrome extension. Firefox and Safari are not supported for browser automation in Claude Code.

faq.question: What happens if automation fails?
faq.answer: Every failure shows a manual fallback prompt you can copy-paste into Lovable. Automation never blocks progress.

faq.question: Can I disable auto-deploy after git push?
faq.answer: Yes. Use /yolo --no-auto-deploy to require manual commands while keeping yolo mode on for other features.

faq.question: How does this differ from manual prompts?
faq.answer: Manual prompts require you to copy-paste into Lovable. Yolo mode handles navigation, submission, and verification automatically.
